description_long: This is a classic wooden barrel with a slightly bulging midsection, constructed from dark, vertically aligned wooden staves. The staves are tightly bound by three wide, black metal hoops that encircle the barrel at the top, middle, and bottom, providing structural reinforcement and a rustic appearance. The wood surface is textured, showing visible grain and a weathered finish, suggesting durability and age. The top of the barrel is flat and closed, with no visible openings or handles, and the ends of the staves are neatly aligned. The metal bands have a slightly worn, matte finish, indicating exposure to the elements or frequent handling. This type of barrel is commonly found in urban settings such as alleys, courtyards, or storage areas, often used for storage or decorative purposes.
description: A dark wooden barrel with three black metal hoops, a flat closed top, and a weathered, textured surface. The barrel has a classic bulging shape and no visible handles or markings.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full bulging profile, three metal hoops, and the flat closed top.
description_view_1: Slightly rotated view highlighting the curvature of the staves and the texture of the wood.
description_view_2: Another angled view with clear visibility of the metal hoops and the even alignment of the staves.
description_view_3: Rear view similar to the front, showing the same structural features and banding.
